test/app.purpose: Integration tests for app integrations (Stripe, custom invoicing) — validates marketplace listing lifecycle, invoice sync, and async invoice state protocols against a real Postgres DB without importing app/common wiring.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface + testEnv struct
patterns.description: Each sub-package defines a TestEnv interface exposing domain services (App(), Adapter(), etc.) and a concrete testEnv struct implementing it. NewTestEnv wires all services from raw package constructors (appadapter.New, appservice.New, etc.).
patterns.example: type TestEnv interface { App() app.Service; Adapter() app.Adapter; Close() error }

patterns.name: Suite struct with Env field
patterns.description: Test logic is grouped in a *HandlerTestSuite struct embedding Env TestEnv and a namespace string. Each test method calls setupNamespace(t) at the top to get an isolated ULID namespace.
patterns.example: type AppHandlerTestSuite struct { Env TestEnv; namespace string }

patterns.name: Raw package constructors only
patterns.description: testenv.go wires all services using direct package constructors (appadapter.New, billingadapter.New, billingservice.New, etc.) and never imports app/common or Wire provider sets.

patterns.name: migrate.Up() in NewTestEnv
patterns.description: Each test environment runs migrate.Up() on the pgtestdb-provisioned Postgres connection before constructing services, ensuring schema is current without relying on autoMigrate config.

patterns.name: InitBillingService helper function
patterns.description: In test/app, billing service construction is extracted into InitBillingService(t, ctx, InitBillingServiceInput) to allow reuse across different test files in the same package.
patterns.example: billingService, err := InitBillingService(t, ctx, InitBillingServiceInput{DBClient: entClient, CustomerService: customerService, AppService: appService})

key_file_guides.role: Constructs the full service graph for app integration tests. Defines TestEnv interface, testEnv struct, NewTestEnv constructor, and InitBillingService helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never import app/common here. Always call defer migrator.CloseOrLogError(). closerFunc must close both entClient and driver.EntDriver.

key_file_guides.role: AppHandlerTestSuite with test methods for GetMarketplaceListing and ListMarketplaceListings.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always call setupNamespace(t) at the top of each test method.

key_file_guides.role: Test entry point; creates TestEnv, defers Close(), then runs sub-tests via AppHandlerTestSuite.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ctx cancel must be deferred. env.Close() errors must be reported via t.Errorf.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or Wire provider sets in testenv.go — always wire from raw package constructors
Sharing a namespace across test methods in AppHandlerTestSuite — always call setupNamespace(t) per method
Calling Stripe client methods directly instead of going through app.Service or appstripe.Service
Forgetting defer stripeClient.Restore() / stripeAppClient.Restore() in stripe sub-tests
Adding invoice line assertions without stable-sorting before setting mock expectations

decisions.decision: InitBillingService is a shared helper rather than inline construction
decisions.rationale: Multiple test files (app_test.go, invoice_test.go) need a billing.Service; extracting the constructor avoids duplication and keeps testenv.go focused on the app-level wiring.

decisions.decision: Two parallel test architectures: TestEnv interface (app_test.go) vs billingtest.BaseSuite embedding (stripe/invoice_test.go)
decisions.rationale: Simple marketplace tests need only an App() and Adapter(), while invoice sync tests need the full billing suite machinery from billingtest.BaseSuite.

code_examples.scenario: Wire a minimal test environment from raw constructors

test/customer.purpose: Integration tests for customer, subject, subscription, entitlement, plan, and billing domains wired together — validates cross-domain lifecycle correctness (usage attributions, subject hooks, subscription constraints, invoice generation) against real Postgres.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface with all cross-domain services
patterns.description: TestEnv exposes Customer(), Subscription(), SubscriptionWorkflow(), Entitlement(), Feature(), Subject(), Plan(), Billing(), App(), and Meter() so test methods can exercise multi-domain flows without constructing services inline.
patterns.example: type TestEnv interface { Customer() customer.Service; Subscription() subscription.Service; Subject() subject.Service; ... }

patterns.name: Hooks registered after service construction
patterns.description: After building customerService and subjectService, cross-domain hooks are wired manually: customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(entValidator), subjectService.RegisterHooks(subjectCustomerHook), customerService.RegisterHooks(customerSubjectHook), customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(subsCustValidator).

patterns.name: subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps for DB bootstrapping
patterns.description: NewTestEnv uses subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps(t) instead of testutils.InitPostgresDB directly, which handles schema migration and returns a DBClient ready for all adapters.

patterns.name: Unique ULID namespace per test method via setupNamespace(t)
patterns.description: Every test method in CustomerHandlerTestSuite begins with s.setupNamespace(t) which assigns a new ulid.Make().String() to s.namespace, providing full test isolation.

patterns.name: clock.SetTime + t.Cleanup(clock.ResetTime) for time-sensitive tests
patterns.description: Tests that need to advance time call clock.SetTime(future) and always register t.Cleanup(clock.ResetTime) to prevent global clock state from leaking between tests.

key_file_guides.role: Constructs the full multi-domain service graph. Wires meter, customer, entitlement, subject, plan, app, subscription, workflow, and billing from raw package constructors. Registers all hooks post-construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hook registration order matters: entValidator before subjectHook before subscriptionValidator. Never import app/common. noopCustomerOverrideService is an inline noop for billing.CustomerOverrideService.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerHandlerTestSuite with CRUD tests for customer lifecycle, key conflict detection, and subscription-present update constraints.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UsageAttribution is nil (not empty slice) when no subjects are attached — assertions must check Nil not Empty.

key_file_guides.role: Tests subject deletion edge cases (dangling subjects, subjects still attached to customers, subjects with active entitlements) and TestMultiSubjectIntegrationFlow — a full plan+subscription+invoice end-to-end.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestMultiSubjectIntegrationFlow installs a sandbox app and creates a billing profile — both must succeed or the test panics. clock.ResetTime must be deferred after clock.SetTime.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: creates TestEnv, defers Close(), runs Customer and Subject sub-test groups.
key_file_guides.watch_for: t.Errorf used for Close errors (not t.Fatal) to ensure deferred Close always runs.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or Wire provider sets — all wiring must use raw package constructors to avoid import cycles
Using context.Background() in new test code where t.Context() is available
Reusing the same subject key or customer key across test functions — always generate unique keys per test
Forgetting to defer clock.ResetTime() after clock.SetTime() — leaves global clock dirty for subsequent tests
Asserting UsageAttribution as empty slice instead of nil — service returns nil when there are no subject keys

decisions.decision: TestEnv exposes all cross-domain services (10 methods) rather than just customer
decisions.rationale: Subject deletion, subscription constraints, and multi-subject billing flows require real instances of subscription, entitlement, billing, and plan — a minimal customer-only env cannot exercise these paths.

decisions.decision: Hooks wired manually in NewTestEnv rather than discovered via DI
decisions.rationale: Tests must stay independent from app/common. Manual hook registration ensures the exact hook set used in tests matches production wiring without importing Wire providers.
Centralises pgtestdb provisioning and schema migration so multiple test packages share the same proven setup path, reducing per-package boilerplate.

code_examples.scenario: Multi-domain integration test: create customer, subscribe, invoice

test/entitlement.purpose: Structural parent for entitlement integration and regression test packages — owns the test/entitlement/regression sub-package that guards grant burn-down, expiry, voiding, and reset edge cases against real Postgres.
anti_patterns: Adding source files directly in test/entitlement — all code belongs in the regression sub-package
Importing app/common or Wire provider sets from any test under this folder

decisions.decision: Regression tests are isolated in a sub-package rather than co-located with production code
decisions.rationale: Keeps the regression harness separate from openmeter/entitlement/ domain code, allowing test-only imports (pgtestdb, MockStreamingConnector) without polluting the production package graph.
